cover.status_line: Confidential · For Internal Algolia Use Only

company_snapshot.revenue_source_label: Tapestry IR — FY2025 Full Year Results

executives.name: Joanne Crevoiserat
executives.title: Chief Executive Officer, Tapestry Inc.
executives.quote: Tapestry is a consumer-obsessed, data-driven organization, driving meaningful durable growth.
executives.relevance: CEO positions data obsession as core to strategy — yet coach.com search returns 1 result for 'gift for mom under 300', a failure that any truly data-driven team would surface immediately.

executives.name: Joanne Crevoiserat
executives.title: Chief Executive Officer, Tapestry Inc.
executives.quote: Tapestry is applying AI tools across the value chain — product development, inventory management, pricing, and marketing — and described a focus on putting tools 'in the hands of the decision-makers.'
executives.relevance: AI across the value chain — but site search, which drives $345M of addressable revenue, remains keyword-only with no semantic AI layer.

executives.name: Yang Lu
executives.title: Chief Information & Digital Officer, Tapestry Inc.
executives.quote: Digital will continue to be really important for us, because at the end of the day, we want to meet our customers where they are, and our customer is an omnichannel customer. [Success requires] persistent, relentless experimentation and building a culture of test and learn. Iteration is the new perfection.
executives.relevance: Yang advocates relentless experimentation — yet there is no evidence of A/B testing on search. Search is a static keyword engine, not an iterating AI system.

executives.name: Fabio Luzzi
executives.title: Chief Data & Analytics Officer, Tapestry Inc.
executives.quote: This marks an important milestone in Tapestry's journey to make data and AI a true competitive advantage. Over the past few years, we've built a world class data foundation — culminating in our patented Data Fabric, which powers everything from advanced analytics to AI and GenAI across our brands.
executives.quote_source_label: Retail Tech Innovation Hub — CDAO Appointment
executives.relevance: Data Fabric powers AI across brands — but site search feeds no signal back into that fabric. Search is a blind spot in Tapestry's data ecosystem.

executives.name: Fabio Luzzi
executives.title: Chief Data & Analytics Officer, Tapestry Inc.
executives.quote: What's changing is how data and AI are helping us do it — amplifying creativity with greater insight, and speed. The journey continues, and we're just getting started.
executives.quote_source_label: Retail Tech Innovation Hub — CDAO Appointment
executives.relevance: AI amplifying creativity — Algolia NeuralSearch would amplify the customer's search creativity: understanding 'gift for mom under 300' the way a knowledgeable store associate would.

executives.name: Noam Paransky
executives.title: Chief Omni and Innovation Officer, Tapestry Inc.
executives.quote: Amperity allows us to better understand our customers and put actionable insights into the hands of our teams to drive experimentation and activation, enabling us to personalize the critical touchpoints our brands have with our customers without the need for significant technology expertise or code.
executives.quote_source_label: Consumer Goods Technology — Tapestry Personalized Experiences
executives.relevance: Noam invested in no-code personalization via Amperity. Algolia's visual merchandising rules dashboard gives the same 'no-code' control to search — the same philosophy, applied to the discovery layer.

executives.name: Noam Paransky
executives.title: Chief Omni and Innovation Officer, Tapestry Inc.
executives.quote: Every customer touchpoint has an opportunity to make a positive, neutral or negative impression on a customer. The most important channel is the channel that the customer is engaging with now and today, so embrace it. Seize that moment.
executives.quote_source_label: Total Retail — CDO on Digital Marketing Dos and Don'ts
executives.relevance: When a customer types 'gift for mom under 300' and gets 1 irrelevant result, that touchpoint makes a negative impression. This is the exact failure Noam describes — happening on site search today.

executives.name: Sandeep Seth
executives.title: President, Global Consumer and Innovation (Chief Growth Officer), Tapestry Inc.
executives.quote: The most likely chance of getting a second date is how good the first date was. It's not how many times you call them back or send them texts. That's the way people feel about brands.
executives.relevance: Sandeep's 'first date' analogy is powerful. If a new Gen Z customer's first search returns zero relevant results, how many second dates is Tapestry losing every day?

executives.name: Scott Roe
executives.title: Chief Financial Officer and Chief Operating Officer, Tapestry Inc.
executives.quote: Sending 13 emails after someone visits your store doesn't build loyalty. It's about making the first interaction meaningful, then communicating only when we have something relevant to say. 'Magic and logic' — where merchant instinct is backed by machine learning.
executives.relevance: Scott describes 'magic and logic' — merchant instinct + ML. Algolia is exactly that: a merchandising rules layer (magic) over an AI-ranked relevance engine (logic).

executives.name: Todd Kahn
executives.title: CEO and Brand President, Coach
executives.quote: For a long time, fashion ads were a model with a bag, and then another model with a bag. We anchor our storytelling in something much more profound, like confidence and self-expression, and that's what creates desire. If you're just [focusing] on product, but the environment isn't inviting for Gen Z, you're not going to capture them.
executives.relevance: Todd says an uninviting environment loses Gen Z. When Gen Z types a natural language search and gets 1 result, the digital environment is the opposite of inviting.

intelligence_signals.text: Joanne Crevoiserat, CEO: 'Tapestry is a consumer-obsessed, data-driven organization.' But coach.com search returns 1 result for 'gift for mom under 300' — a failure that contradicts the data-obsessed brand promise.
intelligence_signals.source_name: Joanne Crevoiserat, CEO — Tapestry Amplify Investor Day
intelligence_signals.relevance: Urgent credibility gap: the CEO's data-obsession pledge is undermined daily by failed NLP queries on the flagship e-commerce site.

intelligence_signals.text: Tapestry is applying AI across product development, inventory management, pricing, and marketing (Q2 FY2026 earnings). CapEx + cloud investment: $200M planned for FY2026, 40% to IT and digital.
intelligence_signals.source_name: Joanne Crevoiserat, CEO — Q2 FY2026 Earnings Call
intelligence_signals.relevance: $80M of $200M CapEx is for IT/digital. Active technology investment cycle — Algolia should be in the conversation now.

intelligence_signals.text: Tapestry partnered with Lily AI specifically to 'cut down on failed searches.' This confirms search quality is a known internal pain point with executive visibility.
intelligence_signals.relevance: Lily AI enriches product attributes but does NOT solve query-side NLP, typo tolerance, or federated search. Algolia fills the gaps Lily AI cannot.

intelligence_signals.text: Fabio Luzzi, CDAO: 'We've built a world class data foundation — culminating in our patented Data Fabric, which powers everything from advanced analytics to AI and GenAI across our brands.' (Nov 2025)
intelligence_signals.source_name: Fabio Luzzi, CDAO — Retail Tech Innovation Hub
intelligence_signals.relevance: Data Fabric is ready. Search analytics from Algolia would feed first-party behavioral signals back into the fabric — a compelling integration story for Fabio Luzzi.

intelligence_signals.text: Tapestry digital revenue grew +20% YoY in Q2 FY2026. DTC revenue +17%. E-commerce is now ~35% of total $7B+ revenue. 6.8 million new customers acquired in FY2025.
intelligence_signals.relevance: 20% digital growth means more queries hitting a below-average search engine. Every point of growth amplifies the cost of search failure. Scale urgency is real and growing.

intelligence_signals.text: 60% of new Coach customer acquisitions are Gen Z and Millennials. Gen Z expects conversational, natural language search. Coach.com currently returns 1 result for NLP queries like 'gift for mom under 300'.
intelligence_signals.source_name: Digital Commerce 360 — Tapestry FY2025 Earnings Recap
intelligence_signals.relevance: The very customers driving Tapestry's growth have the highest search quality expectations. NLP failures on Gen Z queries are a direct threat to the acquisition strategy.

intelligence_signals.text: ZERO of Coach's 5 direct competitors use Algolia. Michael Kors uses Oracle Endeca, Tory Burch / Marc Jacobs / Furla use SFCC Einstein, Longchamp uses Shopify native. Tapestry could be FIRST in accessible luxury handbags to adopt AI-native search.
intelligence_signals.source_name: Phase 1 Competitor Research — BuiltWith + Pivotree + Salesforce sources
intelligence_signals.relevance: Golden angle: first-mover advantage. 70% of luxury brands use Algolia. Coach's peer set is not among them — yet.

intelligence_signals.text: No Search Engineer, Relevance Engineer, or Elasticsearch roles open at Tapestry (751 open roles analyzed, Feb 2026). Zero in-house search team = confirmed buy-not-build orientation.
intelligence_signals.relevance: No competing internal build project. Tapestry's talent investment is in digital strategy, site experience, and personalization — all categories where Algolia's SaaS model fits directly.

intelligence_signals.text: VP, eCommerce Kate Spade (open) — $424M revenue stream. Sr. Director, Site Experience Outlet eCommerce (open) — $1B+ business. Both roles are prime Algolia entry points.
intelligence_signals.relevance: New hires in VP eCommerce and Sr. Director Site Experience = 90-day window to shape their search technology roadmap before they commit to status quo.

intelligence_signals.text: Tapestry plans 60+ new stores in China across 100+ cities (up from 400+ stores in 90 cities). China digital grew 19% YoY in Q1 FY2026.
intelligence_signals.relevance: China expansion requires multi-language search with Chinese character support, localized synonyms, and regional merchandising — all capabilities of Algolia's global infrastructure.

intelligence_signals.text: Tapestry FY2025 revenue: $7.01B (+5% YoY), gross margin 75.4% (+210 bps), Non-GAAP EPS $5.10. Full-year guidance raised to ~$7.2B for FY2026. Heading toward $10B by 2028 target.
intelligence_signals.source_name: Tapestry IR — FY2025 Full Year Results
intelligence_signals.relevance: Financial health is strong: $1.35B free cash flow, $1.12B cash. Budget is available for enterprise search investment aligned to the $10B growth trajectory.

intelligence_signals.text: Browser audit confirmed: 'gift for mom under 300' → 1 result (Sammy Watch Gift Set, $195). Search keyword-matched on 'gift' only — zero NLP, zero intent detection, zero price-range filtering for conversational queries.
intelligence_signals.relevance: This is not hypothetical — observed live during audit. With 13.5M monthly visitors and 60% Gen Z acquisition mix, this failure happens thousands of times per day.

competitors.name: Michael Kors
competitors.search_vendor: Oracle Endeca + Shopping Muse AI overlay
competitors.notes: Oracle ATG headless architecture. Shopping Muse AI assistant for conversational search. Direct accessible luxury competitor. Oracle Endeca is legacy tech (acquired 2011).

competitors.name: Tory Burch
competitors.notes: SFCC native Einstein search. Same platform as Coach. Launched on Demandware (now SFCC) in 2011. Comparable search limitations.

competitors.name: Marc Jacobs
competitors.notes: SFCC native. Part of LVMH portfolio. LVMH standardization limits search innovation.

competitors.name: Furla
competitors.notes: Migrated to SFCC in September 2022. Italian luxury leather goods. Native SFCC search still maturing post-migration.

competitors.name: Longchamp
competitors.notes: Shopify native search — weakest of all competitors. No AI capabilities. French luxury accessible brand. Le Pliage competes with Coach totes.

findings.title: Zero NLP: 1 Result for Conversational Query
findings.expected_behavior: 100+ gift-appropriate products under $300 (handbags, wallets, scarves, accessories) with intent-aware filtering
findings.actual_behavior: Only 1 result returned: 'Sammy Watch Gift Set, 22.5 Mm X 28 Mm - $195'. Search keyword-matched on 'gift' only. Zero understanding of gift occasion, recipient, or price constraint in complex NLP context.
findings.impact_stat: 1 result for multi-intent query vs. expected 100+. 60% of new Coach customers are Gen Z/Millennials who expect conversational search.
findings.prospect_description: When a customer types 'gift for mom under 300,' coach.com returns one watch. A store associate would immediately show hundreds of beautiful options — the website cannot.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia NeuralSearch with hybrid keyword + vector search. Understands occasion ('gift for mom'), recipient context, and price constraints simultaneously. Returns semantically relevant results without keyword matching.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: +150% sales contribution from search; +37% conversion rate; +210% search usage

findings.title: No-Results Page Shows Irrelevant Products
findings.expected_behavior: A helpful zero-results state with popular products, search suggestions, and guidance
findings.actual_behavior: Returns 4 random, irrelevant products: Signature Rainbow Print Silk Square Scarf ($95), Restored Sleeveless Pleated Dress ($395), Bone Single Earring ($95), Sterling Silver Double Charm Long Pendant ($255). No 'Did you mean?' guidance, no popular searches.
findings.impact_stat: 12% of all e-commerce searches return zero results; 75% of users abandon after a no-results page.
findings.prospect_description: When a customer misspells or uses an unusual term, coach.com shows random jewelry and dresses instead of helping them find what they need. 3 out of 4 customers leave and don't come back.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Query Suggestions with zero-results recovery. Dynamic display of popular searches, trending products, and 'Did you mean?' corrections. Business rules can pin specific products to the zero-results state.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: +150% sales contribution; -88% bounce rate on search

findings.title: Zero On-Site Personalization in Search Results
findings.expected_behavior: Personalized ranking based on browsing history, purchase history, and user segment — VIP customers see different results than first-time visitors
findings.actual_behavior: Static keyword results with no personalization signals detected. Facets present (Categories, Bag Categories, Gender, Price, Color, Bag Size, Percent Off, Size, Material) but no user-context-aware ranking. Bluecore handles email personalization only — no on-site search personalization.
findings.impact_stat: Personalization drives 15–20% conversion lift in luxury e-commerce. Coach's 6.8M new customers receive identical search results regardless of profile.
findings.prospect_description: A returning Coach VIP who has purchased 5 Tabby bags sees the same search results as a first-time visitor. Amperity CDP holds her complete profile — but search ignores all of it.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Personalization + Recommend API. Connects Amperity CDP profiles to real-time search ranking. VIP customers surface different relevance weights. Works without code changes for merchandisers.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: +150% order rate increase and +32% add-to-cart on Black Friday via Algolia Recommend

findings.title: Strong Typo Tolerance — 565 Results for Misspell
findings.expected_behavior: Return relevant handbag results despite typo
findings.actual_behavior: 565 results returned for 'handbagg'. Products include Brooklyn Shoulder Bag ($395), Chelsea Shoulder Bag ($325), Juliet Shoulder Bag ($375), Plaza Bag ($125). No empty state, no error message.
findings.impact_stat: 565 relevant results for misspelled query — demonstrates SFCC has functional fuzzy matching, possibly enhanced by Lily AI attribute enrichment.
findings.prospect_description: Coach handles simple typos well — a customer who types 'handbagg' still finds relevant products. This is a strong baseline to build on.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia's built-in typo tolerance with configurable edit distance and per-field settings would maintain this strength while adding NLP on top.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: +150% sales contribution from search with Algolia's full feature set

findings.title: Federated Search Redirects to Help Content
findings.expected_behavior: Surface returns help content, not just product results
findings.actual_behavior: Redirected directly to full Returns & Exchanges help page. Displays Order Status, Returns & Exchanges, Coach Cares, FAQ sections with complete policy table and timeframes.
findings.impact_stat: Non-product content (return policies, FAQs, store locator) is indexed and searchable — basic content-commerce integration confirmed.
findings.prospect_description: When customers need help (not products), coach.com correctly surfaces the returns page. Basic federated search is working.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Federated Search would extend this to surface products, stores, editorial content, and help articles in a unified SAYT panel — not just redirect to one content type.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: +150% sales contribution with full federated search implementation

findings.title: Fast SAYT with Product Thumbnails
findings.actual_behavior: 'TOP PRODUCTS' dropdown with product thumbnails and 'Top Searches' suggestions including Handbag and Handbags appear within <200ms. Visual SAYT with image previews confirmed functional.
findings.impact_stat: Sub-200ms SAYT response with product images — acceptable performance for luxury retail standard.
findings.prospect_description: The search-as-you-type experience is fast and shows product images — a good foundation to build on.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia's <50ms global search latency would further improve this, with richer SAYT including editorial content, trending queries, and personalized suggestions.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: Sub-50ms response globally with +210% search usage increase

findings.title: Empty State Shows Popular Searches
findings.actual_behavior: 'BEST-SELLING PRODUCTS' section on left + 'Popular Searches' on right: Wallet, Tote, Shoulder Bag, Tabby, Brooklyn. Good UX guidance for new visitors.
findings.impact_stat: Popular searches shown in empty state — correct UX pattern to guide new visitors toward high-intent queries.
findings.prospect_description: When a customer clicks search without typing, coach.com intelligently shows best-sellers and popular searches — a strong starting experience.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Query Suggestions with trending queries, personalized suggestions based on browsing history, and seasonally-adjusted recommendations would make this even more powerful.

findings.title: Mobile Search Functional but Lacks Gen Z Features
findings.expected_behavior: Full-featured mobile search with responsive results and easy filtering
findings.actual_behavior: Responsive mobile design confirmed. Mobile search functional with basic product grid. No evidence of mobile-specific optimizations: no voice search, no visual search, no swipe-to-filter.
findings.impact_stat: Gen Z and Millennial shoppers (60% of new Coach customers) are predominantly mobile-first. Mobile search lacks capabilities expected by this demographic.
findings.prospect_description: Mobile search works but doesn't delight — no voice search, no visual search, no touch-optimized filtering for the Gen Z customer segment driving Tapestry's growth.
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia InstantSearch React Native / Swift / Kotlin SDKs provide mobile-native search with visual and voice capabilities.
findings.algolia_case_study_result: +314% mobile search usage increase after Algolia implementation

priority_to_product.their_priority: Cut down on failed searches (Lily AI partnership mandate)
priority_to_product.discovery_question: We noticed you partnered with Lily AI to reduce failed searches. What's the current failed-search rate, and are you seeing the lift you expected on coach.com?

priority_to_product.their_source: Joanne Crevoiserat — Tapestry Amplify Strategy, Sep 2025
priority_to_product.discovery_question: Joanne mentioned being 'consumer-obsessed and data-driven.' How are you currently using search analytics to understand what customers search for and don't find?

priority_to_product.their_priority: Tools in the hands of decision-makers without code (Noam Paransky, 2023)
priority_to_product.algolia_product: Algolia Rules Engine + Visual Merchandising Dashboard
priority_to_product.discovery_question: Noam talked about 'no-code' empowerment. How empowered are your merchandising teams today to adjust search results without engineering tickets?
Your team emphasized personalizing 'critical touchpoints.' Is site search one of those touchpoints where you're delivering personalized results today, or is that a gap?

priority_to_product.their_priority: AI across value chain — 'in hands of decision-makers' (CEO, Q2 FY2026)
priority_to_product.their_source: Joanne Crevoiserat — Q2 FY2026 Earnings Call, Feb 2026
priority_to_product.discovery_question: Joanne mentioned AI across product, inventory, pricing, and marketing. Is search and discovery part of that AI roadmap, or is it still keyword-based?

priority_to_product.their_priority: Meet customers where they are — omnichannel (Yang Lu, NRF 2024)
priority_to_product.their_source: Yang Lu — NRF 2024 Unified Commerce Panel
priority_to_product.discovery_question: Yang mentioned meeting customers where they are. When a customer searches on coach.com, can they discover content, store inventory, and help articles in one search, or only products?

priority_to_product.their_priority: 'Iteration is the new perfection' — culture of test and learn (Yang Lu, NRF 2024)
priority_to_product.their_source: Yang Lu — NRF 2024 Unified Commerce Panel
priority_to_product.discovery_question: Yang's philosophy is 'iteration is the new perfection.' How frequently are you A/B testing search algorithms today — weekly, monthly, or is that a manual process?

priority_to_product.their_priority: 'Magic and logic' — merchant instinct backed by ML (Scott Roe, 2025)
priority_to_product.discovery_question: Scott mentioned 'magic and logic.' Does your current search let merchandisers layer their expertise on top of AI-driven relevance, or is it one or the other?

anchor_lines: Your CEO said you're 'consumer-obsessed and data-driven.' Your search engine doesn't know what 'gift for mom under 300' means.
You invested in Lily AI to cut failed searches — but Lily AI solves the catalog side. Who's solving the query side?
You have the Data Fabric. You have Amperity. You have Bluecore. The one blind spot is what customers type — and leave.
Zero of your five competitors use Algolia. Coach could be first in accessible luxury to get this right.
5% lift on $345M search-addressable revenue = $17.25M/year. That's an 8-day payback on Algolia's annual cost.

ae_fields.urgency_reason: 20% digital growth means more queries hitting a broken NLP engine. 60% of new customers are Gen Z expecting conversational search. CapEx cycle active ($200M FY2026, 40% digital). No competitors use Algolia — first-mover window open now.
ae_fields.next_step_action: Request 30-minute discovery call with Yang Lu (CIO) or Mandeep Bhatia (SVP Digital Product) — reference NLP failure screenshot and Gen Z acquisition risk
ae_fields.talk_track_opener: I've been reviewing Tapestry's Investor Day materials, and Joanne's vision of being 'consumer-obsessed and data-driven' really resonated with me. I'm curious — when a Gen Z customer types 'gift for mom under 300' on coach.com today, they get one result: a watch. Does that show up in your search analytics?
ae_fields.talk_track_cta: I'd love to run a 2-week proof of concept on one category on coach.com. We'll show you what NeuralSearch does with the exact queries your customers are failing today — no commitment, all upside.

next_steps.title: Discovery Call — Yang Lu or Mandeep Bhatia
next_steps.description: Lead with the NLP failure screenshot ('gift for mom under 300' → 1 result) and the Gen Z acquisition risk angle. Reference Joanne's 'consumer-obsessed' Investor Day language. Ask about current search analytics visibility and failed-search rate.

next_steps.title: Lily AI Positioning Conversation
next_steps.description: Address the Lily AI partnership directly: 'Lily AI solves the catalog side — product attributes and SEO. Algolia solves the query side — understanding what customers mean, not just what they type. They're complementary.' Present the positioning statement from 07-hiring-signals.md.

next_steps.title: Fabio Luzzi (CDAO) Technical Deep-Dive
next_steps.description: Bring Algolia's Data Fabric integration story — search analytics as a signal feed into the patented Tapestry Data Fabric. This is a unique angle that will resonate with the CDAO's mandate to make data a competitive advantage.

next_steps.title: 2-Week POC on Kate Spade or Coach Outlet
next_steps.description: Propose POC on a non-core catalog section (accessories or shoes) first. Demonstrate NeuralSearch on the 'gift for mom under 300' query. Show personalized results using Amperity segments. Deliver before/after conversion comparison.

next_steps.title: ROI Presentation to Scott Roe (CFO/COO)
next_steps.description: Present the $17.25M–$34.5M annual uplift calculation (5–10% lift on $345M addressable). Reference Scott's own 'magic and logic' framing. 8-day payback at moderate scenario. Align to FY2026 CapEx cycle already underway.

competitor_tiers.search_stack: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Einstein (native) — same platform as Coach
competitor_tiers.strategic_implication: SFCC peers — none have upgraded beyond native search. Coach moving to Algolia leapfrogs all three simultaneously.

competitor_tiers.search_stack: Oracle Endeca + Shopping Muse AI overlay (Oracle ATG headless)
competitor_tiers.strategic_implication: Michael Kors has a conversational AI overlay (Shopping Muse) showing 15-20% conversion improvement. But it's a bolt-on, not AI-native. Algolia's unified architecture outperforms bolt-on approaches at scale.

competitor_tiers.search_stack: Shopify Native Search — weakest in the competitive set
competitor_tiers.strategic_implication: Longchamp uses Shopify's basic search — easiest target for search superiority. If Tapestry upgrades to Algolia, the gap vs. Longchamp becomes immediately visible to cross-shopping customers.

positioning_matrix.algolia_capability: NeuralSearch — hybrid keyword + vector, full conversational intent
Real-time ranking from Amperity CDP profiles — no code needed
Single API across products, content, FAQs, store inventory
Visual Rules Editor — no engineering tickets for boosts/pins/buries

competitive_synthesis.scenario_note: 0 of 5 direct Coach competitors currently use Algolia. Tapestry has a clear first-mover window in accessible luxury handbags before a competitor acts. Algolia serves 70% of luxury brands and 75% of top fashion houses — Coach's peer set is not yet among them.
competitive_synthesis.the_ask: 2-week proof of concept on coach.com accessories category. Zero commitment. Full NeuralSearch + Personalization demo on your actual customer queries.

case_studies.relevance_to_tapestry: Lacoste is the closest luxury fashion analog to Coach — premium accessible brand, handbags and accessories, global e-commerce. Same SFCC ecosystem. Lacoste more than doubled global sales from search after Algolia.
case_studies.quote: Algolia is a critical part of the navigation experience on Lacoste platforms globally…it more than doubled our global sales.

case_studies.relevance_to_tapestry: Harry Rosen is a luxury fashion retailer with comparable product complexity (multiple categories, colorways, materials) and clienteling needs. The 360% conversion lift and ability to give business users control without developer dependency mirrors Tapestry's 'no-code' philosophy.
case_studies.quote: What the business could accomplish became way beyond what they thought was possible in the old world.

case_studies.relevance_to_tapestry: Gymshark is a DTC fashion brand with strong Gen Z/Millennial customer base — identical demographic to Coach's fastest-growing segment. Algolia Recommend drove dramatic conversion improvements on the highest-traffic sales period. Tapestry's seasonal gift-gifting peaks (holiday, Valentine's Day) are the same opportunity.
case_studies.quote: We don't have to do very much with Algolia Recommend. With our previous solution, there was so much manual configuration.

methodology: Phase 1 pre-audit research conducted 2026-02-23 using SimilarWeb (traffic/demographics), BuiltWith (technology detection via free-api and domain-lookup), Yahoo Finance MCP (financial statements), WebSearch/WebFetch (executive intelligence, SEC 10-K/10-Q filings, earnings transcripts, competitor analysis). Phase 2 browser-based audit conducted 2026-02-23 using real Chrome with remote debugging on port 9222 to bypass Akamai WAF. 15 screenshots captured and verified on disk. Scoring uses weighted average: HIGH severity = 2x weight, MEDIUM = 1x, LOW = 0.5x. Final score: 47.5 / 10.5 = 4.5/10.
